commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: 22. "Let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us, according as we have hoped in Thee" (ver. 22): let Thy mercy, O Lord, be upon us; for hope confoundeth not, because we have hoped in Thee.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Thee. All-perfect Being, show thy protection to all who trust in thee. (Worthington)
He who wishes to receive much, ought to increase his hopes. (Calmet)
"Who is so full of hope as boldly to say, by my hope measure thy mercy? "(Theodoret)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
